title: Imperial Core
content:
{{Top|leg}} {{Youmay|the faction|the [[Core Worlds/Legends|Core Worlds]], also known as the "Imperial Core"}} {{Organization |image= |name=Imperial Core |type=Imperial splinter faction''[[Cracken's Threat Dossier]]''''[[The Essential Guide to Warfare]]'' |founder= |leader=[[Admiral/Legends|Admiral]] [[Natasi Daala]] |subgroup=[[Imperial Core Navy]] |headquarters= |locations=*[[Deep Core/Legends|Deep Core]] *[[Core Worlds/Legends|Core Worlds]]''[[The Jedi Academy Sourcebook]]'' *[[Colonies/Legends|Colonies]] |formed=[[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]] |founded= |fragmented=[[25 ABY/Legends|25 ABY]] |reorganized= |dissolved= |restored= |affiliation= }} {{Quote|Aren't there still some Imperial strongholds in the Core Systems?''"
"''Sure are.|[[Jaina Solo Fel|Jaina Solo]] and [[Han Solo/Legends|Han Solo]]|''[[Young Jedi Knights: The Lost Ones]]''}} The '''Imperial Core''' was a term used by the [[New Republic/Legends|New Republic]] to designate the remnants of the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]] active in the [[Greater Core|Core]] during the second half of the [[Galactic Civil War/Legends|Galactic Civil War]]. The New Republic mentioned the Imperial Core as a single entity although it encompassed distinct Imperial forces that varied substantially over time, from Imperial loyalists to [[warlord/Legends|warlords]] constantly fighting each others. ==History== {{Quote|One of Imperial Core's current primary goals is to not lose any further territory. The Empire once controlled, in one form or another, all of Known Space. Five years ago the Empire was in command of about one-third of the charted galaxy. Today the Imperial Core and other Imperial fragments make up less than one-quarter of Known Space, with the majority of that space in the heavily fortified Deep Core.|[[Rear Admiral/Legends|Rear Admiral]] [[Kursk Mal'ia]]|}} The term "Imperial Core" was originally used to refer to the highly [[Imperialization/Legends|imperialized]] [[Greater Core|Core]] at the height of the [[Imperial Era/Legends|Imperial Period]].{{JournalCite|7|Into the Core Worlds}} The Imperial Core therefore encompassed all the star systems of the Core. After the [[Death/Legends|death]] of [[Galactic Emperor/Legends|Emperor]] [[Palpatine/Legends|Palpatine]] at the [[Battle of Endor/Legends|Battle of Endor]] in [[4 ABY/Legends|4 ABY]], the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]] began to collapse. Nevertheless, the Empire remained powerful in the Core between 4 ABY and [[6 ABY/Legends|6 ABY]]. The star systems of the Core Worlds and Colonies remained loyal to the [[Imperial Ruling Council/Legends|Imperial Ruling Council]] while the [[Deep Core/Legends|Deep Core]] saw the arrival of Imperial [[Warlord/Legends|warlord]] who established their picket empires in the region. The most notable were [[Lord High Admiral]] [[Blitzer Harrsk]]'s [[Zero Command]] an [[Superior General]] [[Sander Delvardus]]' [[Eriadu Authority]]. In 6 ABY, the [[New Republic/Legends|New Republic]] began its [[Core Campaign|campaign]] to conquer the Core. After the [[Liberation of Coruscant (Galactic Civil War)|liberation]] of the galactic [[Capital/Legends|capital]] of [[Coruscant/Legends|Coruscant]] in [[7 ABY/Legends|7 ABY]], the New Republic replaced the Empire as the dominant galactic government. The Core Worlds and Colonies systems massively joined the New Republic''[[The Essential Atlas]]'' though many refused to join the New Republic. In the next years, the whole ensemble of Deep Core warlords and few remaining Imperial loyalists in Core began to be referred altogether as the Imperial Core. The space military of the Imperial Core was even known as the [[Imperial Core Navy]]. Nevertheless, the forces of the Imperial Core remained divided, in particular the Deep Core warlords who struggled continuously against each other. Imperial loyalists were enormously frustrated to see the warlords waste their considerable resources rather than targeting the New Republic. In [[8 ABY/Legends|8 ABY]], the New Republic captured two of the major remaining Imperial planets in the Core Worlds: [[Anaxes/Legends|Anaxes]] and [[Kuat/Legends|Kuat]]. By [[9 ABY/Legends|9 ABY]], remaining Imperial star systems in the Core Worlds and Colonies included the [[Carida system/Legends|Carida]], [[Rendili system/Legends|Rendili]], [[Chasin system/Legends|Chasin]] and [[Kelada system/Legends|Kelada systems]]. On the other hand, the warlords' picket empires in the Deep Core included, but not exclusively, Lord High Admiral Harrsk's Zero Command, Superior General Delvardus' Eriadu Authority, [[High Admiral]] [[Treuten Teradoc|Teradoc]]'s [[Federated Teradoc Union]] and [[Moff/Legends|Moff]] [[Foga Brill/Legends|Brill]]'s [[Constitutional Protectorate of Prakith]]. In [[10 ABY/Legends|10 ABY]], the Imperial Core, including the Deep Core warlords, was unified by the reborn Emperor Palpatine along with other Imperial fores in the galaxy. Although this [[Imperial Coalition]] chased the New Republic out of the Core and [[Second Battle of Coruscant|reconquered Coruscant]], the imperial alliance was short-lived and quickly disintegrated in a full-scale [[Imperial Civil War]]. After the [[Operation Shadow Hand|defeat]] of the reborn Emperor Palpatine, the New Republic retook control of the Core and the warlords returned to the Deep Core. The planet [[Carida/Legends|Carida]] remained one the last notable Imperial loyalist world in the Imperial Core but was [[Destruction of Carida|destroyed]] in [[11 ABY/Legends|11 ABY]].''[[The New Essential Chronology]]'' In [[12 ABY/Legends|12 ABY]], Imperial [[Admiral/Legends|Admiral]] [[Natasi Daala]] assassinated thirteen Imperial warlords, including the powerful Deep Core warlords Harrsk, Delvardus, Teradoc and [[Malfkla Yzu|Yzu]], during a conference at [[Tsoss Beacon/Legends|Tsoss Beacon]].[[Darksaber (novel)|''Darksaber'']] Daala therefore [[Imperial Reunification|reunified]] the warlords' forces in one single fleet. After the [[Assault on Yavin 4|defeat]] of Daala during a brief and unsuccessful camping against the New Republic, Admiral [[Gilad Pellaeon/Legends|Pellaeon]] took command of the [[United Warlord Fleets]] and made a junction with the Imperial loyalist systems in the [[Rim]] where he created the [[Imperial Remnant]]. Furthermore, Pellaeon salvaged the assassinated warlords' former holdings in the Deep Core from any useful material and vessel and relocated everything to the Imperial Remnant space. The Imperial presence in the Imperial Core was drastically reduced by the actions of Daala and Pellaeon. The few remaining Imperial warlords in the Deep Core, including Moffs Foga Brill and [[Jmanuel Tethys]], would be known as the [[replacement warlord]]s. Although they still controlled a large military, these Imperial warlords were considerably less powerful than the thirteen warlords executed by Admiral Daala. In the next years, the primary goal of the Imperial Core was to not lose more territory. The Deep Core was heavily fortified and hundreds of starships were deployed to patrol the border between the Imperial Core and the New Republic, including [[IPV-1 System Patrol Craft]]s, [[TIE/rc starfighter]]s, [[Immobilizer 418 Interdictor-class Heavy Cruiser|''Interdictor''-class heavy cruisers]], [[Strike-class medium cruiser|''Strike''-class medium cruisers]], [[Adz-class patrol destroyer|''Adz''-class patrol destroyers]] and [[Imperial Patrol Frigate]]s. After the [[Battle of N'zoth|end]] of the [[Black Fleet Crisis]] in [[17 ABY/Legends|17 ABY]], [[Camp Pa'aal Squadron|surviving Imperial forces]] of [[Sixth Negative Regions|Black Sword Command]] escaped the [[Koornacht Cluster/Legends|Koornacht Cluster]] and joined the Imperial Remnant and other Imperial forces. Approximately 60 warships, the equivalent of ten [[superiority fleet]]s including ''Interdictor''-class heavy cruisers, [[Victory-class Star Destroyer/Legends|''Victory''-class Star Destroyers]], [[Imperial-class Star Destroyer/Legends|''Imperial''-class Star Destroyers]] and the [[Super Star Destroyer/Legends|Super Star Destroyer]] [[Intimidator (Executor-class)|''Intimidator'']] joined the Imperial Core Navy. Four ''Victory''-class Star Destroyers joined the Deep Core warlords. In 17 ABY, Admiral Daala unified the Deep Core warlords under her command. The replacement warlords and the [[Council of Moffs/Legends|Council of Moffs]] of the Imperial Remnant then agreed to launch a joint [[Imperial Skirmishes|campaign]] against the New Republic. The campaign was a disaster and forced the Imperial Remnant to negotiate a [[Pellaeon–Gavrisom Treaty|peace treaty]] with the New Republic [[19 ABY/Legends|two years later]]. However, the few surviving replacement warlords rejected these accords.''[[The New Essential Guide to Characters]]'' The Deep Core warlords later founded the [[Second Imperium]]. They pooled their resources to create the [[Shadow Academy]], a training center for [[Dark Jedi/Legends|Dark Jedi]]. The Second Imperium was eventually [[Second Imperium Crisis|defeated]] by the New Republic and the [[New Jedi Order]] in [[23 ABY/Legends|23 ABY]]. In [[25 ABY/Legends|25 ABY]], the New Republic launched a successful [[Attacks on Imperial holdouts|campaign]] to eliminate the remnants of the Second Imperium and the various Imperial warlords that rejected the peace treaty between the Imperial Remnant and the New Republic.[[Starships of the Galaxy (Saga Edition)|''Starships of the Galaxy'', Saga Edition]] By the time of the [[Yuuzhan Vong War]], all the remnants of the Imperial Core had finally been cleared out of the Deep Core.''[[The New Jedi Order: Destiny's Way]]'' ==Appearances== *''[[Jedi Search]]'' {{1st}} *[[Dark Apprentice (novel)|''Dark Apprentice'']] *''[[Champions of the Force]]'' *[[Darksaber (novel)|''Darksaber'']] *''[[The New Jedi Order: Destiny's Way]]'' {{Mo}} ==Sources== *''[[The Jedi Academy Sourcebook]]'' {{1stID|Imperial Core}} *''[[Cracken's Threat Dossier]]'' *''[[The Essential Guide to Warfare]]'' ==Notes and references== {{Reflist}} {{Interlang |es=Núcleo Imperial }} [[Category:Imperial splinter factions]]
